text_fr: RESOLUTIONS ADOPTED ON THE REPORTS  OF THE SECOND COMMITTEE
text_en: RESOLUTIONS ADOPTED ON THE REPORT OF THE SECOND COMMITTEE

text_fr: RESOLUTIONS  ADOPTEES  SUR  LES  RAPPORTS DE  LA  DEUXIEME  COMMISSION
text_en: RESOLUTIONS ADOPTED ON THE REPORTS OF THE SECOND COMMITTEE

text_fr: 45  (1). World  Shortage of Cereals and  other Foodstuffs
text_en: 45 (1). World Shortage of Cereals and Other Foodstuffs

text_fr: At its thirty-third plenary meeting, on  14  Feb› ruary 1946, the General Assembly adopted a reso› lution  urging  action,  both  direc~ly  by  Govern› ments  and  through  the  international  organiza› tions  concerned,  to  alleviate  the  anticipated serious shortage of bread grains and rice.
text_en: At its thirty-third plenary meeting, on 14 February 1946, the General Assembly adopted a reso› light urging action, both directive~ly by Government› ments and through the international organization› tions concerned, to alleviate the anticipated serious shortage of bread grains and rice.

text_fr: The General Assembly has learned with satis› faction  of  the  extent  to  which  the  position  in 194G  was  improved,  particularly  with respect  to bread grains, by  the common effort of the United Nations, thus saving millions of lives  during the critica! months before the 1946 harvest.
text_en: The General Assembly has learned with satis› faction of the exit to which the position in 194G was improved, particularly with respect to bread grains, by the common effort of the United Nations, that saving millions of lives during the critica! months before the 1946 harvest.

text_fr: The  General  Assembly  recognizes,  however, that  the  food  situation  is  still  unsatisfactory.  A number of countries have not yet  overcome the devastating  results  of  the  enemy  occupation  to which  they  were  subjected,  and are  obliged  on this  account  to  continue  emergency  imports  of grains,  fats  and other foodstuffs.  A  severe  short› age  of these  foodstuffs  exists  in  many  European countries, even in sorne of those which before thc war were  themselves  exporters.  In a  number  of countries of Asia the shortage of cereals and other foodstuffs has led to undernourishment and even famine, resulting in heavy loss of human lives, as in the case  of India and China. There is  also  a widespread shortage of livestock.
text_en: The General Assembly recognizes, however, that the food situation is still unsatisfactory. A number of countries have not yet overcome the defeat results of the enemy occupation to which they were subjected, and are obliged on this account to continue emergency imports of grains, fats and other foodstuffs. A few shorts› age of these foodstuffs exist in many European countries, even in sorne of that which is before three war were themself-exports. In a number of countries of Asia the shortage of cerreals and other foodstuffs has led to undernourishment and even famine, resulting in heavy loss of human lives, as in the case of India and China. There is also a width of livestock.

text_fr: The General Assembly notes, moreover, that in 1945 and 1946 sorne countries of Europe and Asia were affected by drought and bad harvest, result› ing  in  still  further  deterioration  of  their  food situation. Sorne  countries which were  not under enemy  occupation  have  even  introduced  bread rationing  for  the  first  time,  for  instance,  the United Kingdom. In addition, sorne countries of Latin  America  are  experiencing  food  shortages and are obliged to import grain.
text_en: The General Assembly notes, moreover, that in 1945 and 1946 sorne countries of Europe and Asia were affected by drought and bad harvest, result› in still further deterioration of their food situation. Sorne countries which were not under enemy occupation have even introduced bread rationing for the first time, for instance, the United Kingdom. In addition, sorne countries of Latin America are experiencing food shortages and are obliged to import grain.

text_fr: The General  Assembly  has  learned with con› cern that expected supplies of bread grains, rice, fats and oils,  dairy products, meat and sugar ap› pear to be substantially inadequate to meet míni› mum requirements· for  human  consumption  in 1947. Many countries, especially those which have suffered from enemy occupation and those which do not produce sufficient foodstuffs  to meet their own requirements, need agricultura! supplies such as  machinery,  implements,  fertilizers,  pesticides and seeds.
text_en: The General Assembly has learned with con› some that expected supplies of bread grains, rice, fat and oils, dairy products, meat and sugar ap› pear to be substantially inadequate to meet míni› mum requirements· for human consumption in 1947. Many countries, especially those which have been removed from enemy occupation and which do not produce sufficient foodstuffs to meet their own requirements, need agricultura! supplies such as machinery, implements, fertilizers, pesticides and seeds.

text_fr: In addition,  international  payment difficulties
text_en: In addition, international payment difficulties

text_fr: 45  ( 1).  Pénurie mondiale des céréales et autres  denrées alimentaires
text_en: 45 ( 1). World shortage of cereals and other foodstuffs

text_fr: Lors de  sa  trente-troisieme  séance pléniere, le 14  février 194G,  l' Assemblée générale a adopté une résolution demandant  instamment,  et  aux gou› vernements  et  aux organisations  internationales intéressés, de  prendre des  mesures  pour parer a la grave pénurie, alors prévue, de riz et de céréales panifiables.
text_en: At its thirty-third plenary meeting, on 14 February 194G, the General Assembly adopted a resolution urging interested governments and international organizations to take measures to address the projected severe shortage of breadable rice and cereals.

text_fr: L'Assemblée générale a  appris avec satisfaction que  la situation  s'est notablement  améliorée en 1946,  particulierement  en  ce  qui  concerne  les céréales panifiables,  grace  aux  efforts  communs des Nations Unies; on a  réussi ainsi a sauver des millions de vies  au cours des  mois  critiques qui ont précédé la récolte en  1946.
text_en: The General Assembly had learned with satisfaction that the situation had improved significantly in 1946, particularly with regard to bread-making cereals, thanks to the joint efforts of the United Nations; it had succeeded in saving millions of lives in the critical months preceding the harvest in 1946.

text_fr: L'Assemblée générale reconnait cependant que la situation alimentaire reste peu satisfaisante. Un certain nombre de pays subissent encore les consé› quences  désastreuses de  l'occupation ennemie  a laquelle ils ont été soumis, et sont obligés, en con› séquence, de  continuer  leurs  importations  ur› gentes de céréales, de corps gras et d'autres den› rées alimentaires. 11  existe  une grave pénurie de ces  denrées alimentaires dans  beaucoup de  pays d'Europe, meme parmi ceux qui, avant la guerre, exportaient ces  produits. Dans un certain nombre de pays d'Asie, la pénurie de céréales et d'autres dem-ées alimentaires  a  provoqué la sous-alimen› tation  et  meme  la  famine,  et a  eu  pour  consé› quence de lourdes pertes de vies humaines, comme c'est le cas dans l'Inde et en Chine. Une pénurie de  cheptel  s'est  manifestée également dans  de nombreuses régions.
text_en: The General Assembly recognizes, however, that the food situation remains unsatisfactory. A number of countries are still suffering the disastrous consequences of the enemy occupation to which they have been subjected, and are obliged, in con› sequence, to continue their imports ur› gentes of cereals, fatty bodies and other food products. 11 There is a serious shortage of these foodstuffs in many European countries, even among those who, before the war, exported these products. In a number of Asian countries, the shortage of cereals and other food products has led to under-alimentation and even famine, and has resulted in heavy loss of human life, as is the case in India and China.

text_fr: En outre, l'Assemblée générale constate que des pays  d'Europe et  d'Asie ont  connu  en  1945  et 1946 la sécheresse et les mauvaises récoltes, ce qui a  encore aggravé leur situation alimentaire. Cer› tains pays qui n'ont pas été occupés par l'ennemi ont meme établi le rationnement du pain pour la premiere  fois,  le,  Royaume-Uni  par  exemple. D'autre part, plusieurs pays de l'Amérique latine connaissent une pénurie de denrées alimentaires et sont obligés d'importer des céréales.
text_en: In addition, the General Assembly notes that countries in Europe and Asia experienced drought and poor harvests in 1945 and 1946, which further aggravated their food situation. Some countries that were not occupied by the enemy have even established the rationing of bread for the first time, such as the United Kingdom. On the other hand, several countries in Latin America are experiencing food shortages and are forced to import cereals.

text_fr: L'Assemblée générale a  appris avec inquiétude que  la  production  des  céréales panifiablcs,  riz, huiles et corps gras,  produits laitiers,  viandes et sucre, s'annonce  nettement  insuffisante  pour faire  face  aux  besoins  mínima de  la consomma› tion  humaine  en  1947.  De  nombreux  pays,  sur› tout  ceux  qui  ont  souffert  de  l'occupation ennemie et ceux qui ne  produisent pas  suffisam› ment  de  denrées alimentaires  pour  suffire  aux besoins  de  leurs  populations,  réclament  des fournitures  agricoles  telles que machines,  outih, engrais,  insecticides  et  semences.
text_en: The General Assembly has learned with concern that the production of bread, rice, oils and fats, dairy products, meat and sugar is expected to be clearly insufficient to meet the needs of human consumption in 1947. Many countries, of all those who have suffered from enemy occupation and those who do not produce enough food to meet the needs of their populations, demand agricultural supplies such as machinery, outih, fertilizers, insecticides and seeds.

text_fr: De plus,  la  difficulté que certains pays éprou-
text_en: In addition, the difficulty some countries face in

text_fr: the  part  of  certain  importing  countries,  as well as transport and other difficulties,  threaten to  prevent  the  utilization of such food  supplies as may be available.
text_en: the part of certain importing countries, as well as transport and other difficulties, threats to prevent the use of such food supplies as may be available.

text_fr: At the same time, there is  a  tendency  in sorne countries  to  reduce  the  areas  undcr  cultivation of cereals and other foodstuffs,  which may cause unwarranted  price  increascs  and  still  further aggravate the food situation.  Inflationary prices, and other price factors,  in many cases  constitute another obstacle to the production and distribu› tion of food supplies to  those in need.
text_en: At the same time, there is a trend in sorne countries to reduce the areas undcr cultivation of cereals and other foodstuffs, which may cause unwarranted price increases and still further aggravate the food situation. Inflationary prices, and other prices factors, in many cases constitute another obstacle to the production and distribution of food supplies to these in need.

text_fr: The General  Assembly, therefore,
text_en: The General Assembly, therefore,

text_fr: Urges the Governments and international agen› cies  concerned  to  adopt  or  continue  measures designed to overcome  the deficit during  1947  in bread grains,  rice,  fats  and  oils,  dairy  products, meat  and  sugar  and  to  achieve  the  equitable allocation and prompt distribution of  the  avail› able  supplies  free  from  political  considerations; and, in particular,
text_en: Urges the Governments and international agen› cies concerned to adopt or continue measures designed to overcome the deficit during 1947 in bread grains, rice, fat and oils, dairy products, meat and sugar and to achieve the equitable allocation and prompt distribution of the available›able supplies free from political considerations; and, in particular,

text_en: It is not possible to obtain foreign currencies to pay for their imports, insufficient means of transpon and other difficulties that have led to a reduction in the use of available food products.

text_en: At the same time, some countries tend to reduce seeded Sllrfaces into cercals and other food products, which can lead to unjustified price increases and aggravate the food situation. Price inflation, as well as some other price makers, are often a new obstacle to food production and distribution among those who have been cared for.

text_en: The General Assembly, accordingly,

text_fr: 46  ( 1}.  Economic  Reconstruction of  Devastated  Areas
text_en: 46 ( 1}. Economic Reconstruction of Devasted Areas

text_fr: The General Assembly,
text_en: The General Assembly,

text_fr: Taking note of the  preliminary report o(  the Temporary Sub-Commission on Economic Recon› struction of Devastated Areas 1 and o( the relevant resolution  of  the  Economic  and  Social  Council of 3  October  1946 2 ;
text_en: Taking note of the preliminary report o( the Temporary Sub-Commission on Economic Recon› construction of Devasted Areas 1 and o( the relevant resolution of the Economic and Social Council of 3 October 1946 2 ;

text_fr: Recogniz.ing the urgent need for  international co-operation  in  the reconstruction  of devastated areas:
text_en: Recogniz.ing the urgent need for international co-operation in the reconstruction of devastated areas:

items_fr: 1. Approves the general resolution of the Eco› nomic and Social  Council, the resolution on thc Survey of the Economic Reconstruction of Devas› tated Areas in Asia and the Far East, and the reso lution for  continuing the work of  the Sub-Com› mission on Devastated Areas in  Europe 8 ;

2. Urges the Members of the  United Nations, the  Economic and Social  Council,  and  the  spe› cialized  agencies  and inter-governmental  organi› zations concerned, to take ali possible steps, with› in  their  respective  fields  of  activity,  which  may lead to the early solution of the problems of  eco› nomic reconstruction of devastated areas;
3. Directs the  Secretary-General  to  transmi t to  the  International  Bank  for  Reconstruction and  Development  the  opinion  of  the  General
'Document A/147.
· Document A/ 116.
et  a  la  réparation des  machines  agricoles,  de l'outillage et des  pieces de  rechange  les  plus in› dispensables, en vue d'accroitre la production de, vivres;

3.  Que  tous  les  pays  appliquent,  pour autant qu'il  sera  possible,  les  mesures  appropriées et nécessaires en  vue  de réglemeRter la consomma› tion,  y compris:  le  maintien de -taux élevés d'cx› traction, l'adjonction  d'autres  produits  a  la farine,  les  restrictions  a l'utilisation des  céréales panifiables  pour  la  fabrication  de  boissons  et autres  emplois  non  essentiels,  et  les  restrictiom a  l'utilisation  des céréales panifiables  pour l'alimentation des  animaux;

4. Que  les  gouvernements  et  institutions  in› ternationales  intéressés poursuivent  et  dévelop› pent  la  publication  d'informations aussi  détail› lées que  possible  sur  les  approvisionnements  et les  besoins  de  denrées alimentaires  et matériels mentionnés aux  paragraphes  1  et 2  ci-dessus,  et sur  les  mesures  prises  en  exécution des  recom› mandations formulées dans la présente résolution, afin  que  les  décisions  prises  dorénavant s'ap› puient  sur  une  connaissance  complete  des  faits pertinents;

5.  Que l'on ne perde pas de vuela nécessité de prendre les  mesures  indispensables pour permet› tre  aux  pays  importateurs de  surmonter les  dif› ficultés  que  susci tent  les paiements  interna› tionaux,  afin  que  les  recommandations exposées ci-dessus  se  traduisent  par des  résultats eflectifs améliorant la situation alimentaire.
items_en: 1. Approves the general resolution of the Eco› nomic and Social Council, the resolution on thc Survey of the Economic Reconstruction of Devas› tated Areas in Asia and the Far East, and the effects for continuing the work of the Sub-Com› mission on Devasted Areas in Europe 8;

2. Urges the Members of the United Nations, the Economic and Social Council, and the spec› cialized agencies and inter-governmental organizations› zations concerned, to take ali possible steps, with› in their respective fields of activity, which may lead to the early solution of the problems of eco› nomic reconstruction of devastated areas;
3. Directs the Secretary-General to transmit to the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development the opinion of the General
'Document A/147.
· Document A/ 116.
and repair of the most indispensable agricultural machinery, machinery and spare parts, with a view to increasing the production of food;

3. That all countries apply, to the extent possible, appropriate and necessary measures to regulate consumption, including: maintenance of high rates of traction, addition of other products to flour, restrictions on the use of breadable cereals for the manufacture of beverages and other non-essential uses, and restrictions on the use of breadable cereals for animal feed;

4. That interested Governments and international institutions continue and develop the publication of information as detailed as possible› on the supplies and requirements of foodstuffs and materials referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 above, and on the measures taken to implement the recommendations contained in this resolution, so that decisions taken in the future may be informed of the relevant facts;

5. Let us not lose sight of the need to take the necessary steps to enable importing countries to overcome the ficultiated differences in international payments, so that the recommendations outlined above can lead to eflective results that improve the food situation.

text_fr: Cinquante-cinquieme séance pléniere, le  r r  décembre r946.
text_en: Fifty-Fifth Plenary Meeting, R r December r946.

text_fr: 46  ( 1).  Reconstruction économique des régions dévastées
text_en: 46 (1) Economic reconstruction of devastated areas

text_fr: L'Ammwlée générale,
text_en: The General Ammwlea,

text_fr: Prenant acte du rapport préliminaire de la Sous› Commission temporaire de la reconstruction éco› nomique des régions dévastées 1 et de la résolution correspondante  adoptée par  le  Conseil  écouo› mique et social le  3 octobre  1946 2 ;
text_en: Taking note of the preliminary report of the Temporary Committee on the Economic Reconstruction of Desolated Areas 1 and the corresponding resolution adopted by the Economic and Social Council on 3 October 1946; 2

text_en: The urgent need for international cooperation for the reconstruction of the devastated regions:

items_en: 1. Approves the general resolution of the Economic and Social Council, the resolution on the investigation into the economic reconstruction of devastated regions in Asia and the East and the resolution on the continuation of the work of the Sub-Communition of the Devased Regions in Europe;

2. Invites the Members of the United Nations, the Economic and Social Council and the specialized agencies and intergovernmental organizations concerned to take all possible measures, in their respective fields, to achieve a speedy solution to the problems of the economic reconstruction of the devastated regions;
